title: [T3.6] Cancellation-Safe Integration: FS, Process, Signal Flows

proof_requirements.no_feature_behavior: prerequisite declaration only; not behavioral proof

modules_tested.mechanism: synchronous calls or lazy futures with synchronous terminal-completion polls
modules_tested.contract: no async cancellation point inside mutation poll

modules_tested.mechanism: implementation-defined trait contract; UnixVfs delegates to crate::fs boundaries

filesystem_mutation_contracts.cancellation_boundary: a started blocking open may create or truncate after future drop
a started exclusive create may commit after future drop
enabled create or truncate effects may commit after future drop; an unobserved handle is closed
one immediate syscall completes before Poll::Ready; standard partial-write semantics apply
the immediate flush call completes before Poll::Ready
a started durability syscall may finish after future drop
a started resize may commit after future drop
a started permission change may commit after future drop
a started copy may create, truncate, or partially populate the destination after future drop
a submitted rename may commit after future drop
a submitted removal may commit after future drop
a started link creation may commit after future drop
a started or submitted link creation may commit after future drop on supported platforms
a started write may create, truncate, or partially update the target after future drop
staging may finish after future drop, but discarding its result removes the temporary file and never mutates the target
the target rename has no async cancellation point; a later parent-directory sync error is post-commit
future drop can discard staging without changing the target; successful staging is followed in the same poll by synchronous commit
a started or submitted directory creation may commit after future drop
a started traversal may create some or all missing components after future drop
a started or submitted removal may commit after future drop
a started recursive removal may continue after future drop and may leave partial state if it later fails
create and truncate effects complete before the constructor returns
dropping an unpolled lazy future has no effect; one poll drives the write through terminal completion and returns Ready
dropping an unpolled lazy future has no effect; one poll drives the sync through terminal completion and returns Ready
the mutation completes before the function returns
the VfsFile trait makes no rollback guarantee; UnixVfsFile inherits the corresponding File operation boundary
the Vfs trait makes no rollback guarantee; with UnixVfs, enabled create or truncate effects may commit after future drop
the Vfs trait makes no rollback guarantee; each UnixVfs method inherits the matching crate::fs operation-specific boundary
each operation delegates to the matching soft-cancelled crate::fs mutator and may commit after future drop according to that operation's boundary

invariants_proven.description: A started owned File cursor operation completes before subsequent cursor access; its effects are not rolled back
kill_on_drop prevents zombie processes after cancellation
Signal delivery counter survives receiver cancellation
Shutdown broadcast reaches all receivers even if some are cancelled
Cross-module cancellation produces no obligation leaks
A cancelled started direct filesystem write may commit late, while cancelled atomic staging leaves the target unchanged and cleans its temporary file

cross_module_scenarios.behavior: A started write may partially or fully commit; use atomic replacement when required
cross_module_scenarios.leak_risk: No handle leak; no rollback claim

cross_module_scenarios.behavior: Each follows its documented contract; File cursor reuse waits for started owned cursor work

drift_detection.trigger: New async API without cancel-safety annotation
New obligation type without region tracking

no_claims: recovery of an owned read_into_vec buffer after cancellation
atomicity of general writes or path mutations
rollback of a committed StagedAtomicWrite when parent-directory sync reports an error
absence of a temporary file while atomic staging is still in flight
